required_complexity_patterns.notes: Fields inside details/summary or hidden until click
Combobox: button or input with aria-haspopup=listbox plus listbox/options
Multiple controls sharing name attribute groups (checkbox/radio clusters)
At least two fields with aria-label only and no visible label element
Apply form hosted inside iframe or srcdoc iframe shell

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, large, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Fields inside details/summary or hidden until click Required: Combobox: button or input with aria-haspopup=listbox plus listbox/options Required: Multiple controls sharing name attribute groups (checkbox/radio clusters) Required: At least two fields with aria-label only and no visible label element Required: Apply form hosted inside iframe or srcdoc iframe shell Reference DOM (Reverse DOM field order): Input element appears before its label in DOM (float-right layout pattern) Reference DOM (Triple nested fieldsets): Fieldset inside fieldset inside fieldset with legends at each level Reference DOM (Greenhouse progress dots): Greenhouse-inspired multi-section form with progress indicator dots Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: details-accordion, custom-combobox, duplicate-name-attrs, aria-label-only, iframe-apply. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: sidebar + main split apply layout. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Reverse DOM field order; Triple nested fieldsets; Greenhouse progress dots.
